name: Hypertension - Antihypertensives Without HTN Diagnosis (Cross-Indication)
description: Tricky test case: finds patients on antihypertensive drugs who have NO hypertension diagnosis. Antihypertensive drug classes are HEAVILY cross-indication: lisinopril is used for diabetic nephropathy and HF; carvedilol is used for HF; furosemide for edema/HF; amlodipine for angina; losartan for HF or CKD progression. The Synthea module weights this path moderately (~25%) — smaller than GERD's PPI Path C but still substantial.
prompt: I want to find patients in our system who are taking blood pressure medications such as lisinopril, losartan, amlodipine, carvedilol, or furosemide but don't have a formal hypertension diagnosis on their problem list. These medications are commonly prescribed for many other indications.

prompts.naive: Find patients on antihypertensives without an HTN diagnosis.
prompts.expert: I need to identify the antihypertensive cross-indication-prescribing population. Two-step approach: first find patients with MedicationRequest for antihypertensive ingredients (RxNorm 29046 lisinopril, 52175 losartan, 17767 amlodipine, 20352 carvedilol, 4603 furosemide), then exclude patients with a Condition for HTN (SNOMED 59621000, 1201005, 78975002, 429457004, 371125006). The remaining group is on these drugs for HF, CKD, diabetic nephropathy, edema, or angina.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Path C 'tricky' patients: have antihypertensive meds but NO HTN Condition. The Synthea module weights this path moderately (~25%) reflecting widespread cross-indication use.
